title: Where Data Comes From
concept: Data is generated by people, sensors, transactions, and the open web. Knowing the source helps you judge its quality and bias.

exercises.prompt: Recap: photos and voice notes are ___ data?
exercises.explain: Right — they don't fit a tidy table, so they're unstructured.

cards.title: Where does all this data come from?
cards.body: Before AI can use data, something has to generate and capture it. It pours in from a few big sources.

cards.title: People (that's you)
cards.body: Posts, searches, purchases, likes, reviews, app taps. Humans create data with almost every online action.

cards.title: Sensors & devices
cards.body: Phones, smartwatches, cameras, cars, doorbells, satellites — constantly measuring the physical world: location, motion, heart rate, temperature.

cards.title: Transactions & systems
cards.body: Every card swipe, online order, login, and shipment logs data. Businesses quietly run on it.

cards.title: The open web
cards.body: Websites, Wikipedia, news, code, images — scraped at massive scale to train models like ChatGPT (which raises privacy and copyright questions).

cards.title: Two big origins
cards.body: Most data traces back to one of these:

exercises.prompt: Which is data generated by people?
exercises.explain: Posts and likes are human-generated. The rest come from sensors/devices.

exercises.prompt: A smartwatch tracking your heart rate is data from…?
exercises.explain: A heart-rate sensor measures the physical world — sensor/device data.

exercises.prompt: True or false: models like ChatGPT were trained partly on text scraped from the open web.
exercises.explain: True — huge amounts of public web text were used (which is why copyright and privacy come up).

exercises.sentence: Phones, cameras, and smartwatches generate data using ___.
exercises.explain: Sensors capture the physical world — motion, light, sound, location.

exercises.prompt: A team trains an AI using data from only one country's users. Likely risk?

options.text: It may be biased and not work well for everyone else

exercises.explain: Exactly — the source shapes the result. Narrow data → biased AI. We'll cover data bias in the next unit.
